props.message:
THE FORGE IS NOT A KINGDOM.
THE FORGE IS WORK.

Posted bargain:

Heat is given. Shape is owed.
Shape is given. Heat is owed.
No bargain is forgotten.
No bargain is broken.

The Strikers' Memorial:

Kel of the long arm.
Mera of the slow eye.
Bren who could not stop.
Thom who would not start.
Walk well, all of you.

Forge-Law the First:

Do not stand between a spirit and the iron it is working.
The iron does not move aside for you.
The spirit will not.

Forge-Law the Second:

If a spirit speaks, listen.
If a spirit does not speak, listen harder.

A bargain renewed in Brann's hand:

"For the next turning: forty hinges, a kettle, a name we will not say. Acknowledged."

Posted in faded paint:

"Strangers from above: do not bring water close to the smelter pits. The spirits will not warn you twice."

An older inscription, the letters worn:

"The Forge predates the asking of why.
Do your work. Walk well."

props.open_message: A handful of pebbles, warm from the floor.
A heart-flower kept beside the bellows. Still red.
A small iron key. The teeth are still warm.
A fragment of a heart, deep in the Forge. The spirits made room for it.
The Striker's Maul, balanced on a stone anvil at the heart of the Forge. The haft fits as if shaped for your hands.

root.speaker: Forge-master Brann
root.text: You came down. Few do. Be welcome, and step where the iron is cold. What would you ask of the Forge?

what.speaker: Forge-master Brann
what.text: The Forge is older than the asking of questions. They predate wanting. The Wyrdkin came late; the spirits had already been at their work for an age before the first of us learned to listen.

null.speaker: Forge-master Brann
null.text: Null speaks short. Three words at a time, sometimes two. He has agreements with the spirits older than any of us. Do not stand between him and a hammer. Do not knock on his door.

agreements.speaker: Forge-master Brann
agreements.text: The bargains are not written. They are kept. Heat for shape. Shape for heat. The spirits give what is asked, and ask what is owed. We have never been late.

safe.speaker: Forge-master Brann
safe.text: Safe? Hot, mostly. Loud, always. The bone-things you saw at the edge — those are not the spirits. Those are something that tried to bend the spirits and failed. The Forge spat them back out, half-finished.

bye.speaker: Forge-master Brann
bye.text: Walk well, little wanderer. Mind the smelter pits.

root.speaker: Striker Imm
root.text: *Imm sets the hammer down. Wipes their brow.* Forge work is rhythm. You strike to a count, or you don't strike at all.

count.speaker: Striker Imm
count.text: Right, up, right, up, left. Five strikes. The Forge taught me. The Sun-marked gates remember the same five — older than my line, older than the village.

teach.speaker: Striker Imm
teach.text: Then listen. *They tap it out on the anvil.* Right... up... right... up... left. Hum it at any sun-marked gate and it'll open. *[Z] to hum.*

remembered.speaker: Striker Imm
remembered.text: *Imm gives a single approving nod and goes back to the work.*

root.speaker: make
root.text: Got resources? Got a target? I'll build it. What are you building?

target.speaker: make
target.text: Anything you can name. Bomb. Arrow. Compiler. State you don't have yet. Bring the parts; I assemble.

wares.speaker: make
wares.text: Stock on the bench. Bombs, arrows, a compiler if you mean business, a checker if your state's gone foul.

props.message: The first smelter pit — long cooled. Brann the Elder lit the first fire here.

props.warp_message: Smoke parts. The hammers know the foreign cut now.

root.speaker: Anvil Stone
root.text: *A faint hum, like wind through stone…*

hint_pre.speaker: Anvil Stone
hint_pre.text: The Wyrm splits when struck — but the Maul ends a head outright.

hint_post.speaker: Anvil Stone
hint_post.text: The Maul is in your hand. The Wyrm will fall.
